summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #691 in fogodev/ars-ui, specifically fixing registration consistency in navigation menu state helpers and adding missing nested-menu anatomy surfaces defined in the spec.
summary.outcome: Three review findings resolved: added `items_registered` bit to distinguish pre-registration from empty item sets, fixed controlled/stale value handling in state helpers, and added typed `Sub`/`SubList`/`SubItem`/`SubTrigger`/`SubContent`/`SubIndicator`/`SubViewport` parts per spec §5. All local gates passed and changes committed as 62ec346a.

repo: NavigationMenu spec §5 defines a full nested-menu anatomy: Sub, SubList, SubItem, SubTrigger, SubContent, SubIndicator, SubViewport — these must be implemented as typed parts with attrs.

The registration pattern treats an empty registry as 'everything allowed' prior to adapter sync; an explicit `items_registered` flag is needed to distinguish pre-registration state from a deliberately empty item set.
open_item_plan should reject unregistered target keys once registration has occurred — not before.
Snapshot tests for new anatomy parts require INSTA_UPDATE=always on first run to generate fixtures.
Project uses cargo xtask for spec validation and spec TOC inspection (cargo xtask spec validate / cargo xtask spec toc).
CI gates include: formatting (cargo +nightly fmt), snapshot check, workspace check, clippy (-D warnings), and mutual exclusion checks.

code.finding: State helpers for controlled/effective/indicator/viewport values needed an items_registered bit; without it the empty-registry case was indistinguishable from pre-registration, causing stale direct Open events to pass through.
Sub-part anatomy types and attrs were missing; spec §5 requires SubList, SubItem, SubTrigger, SubContent, SubIndicator, SubViewport alongside Sub.
Spec-conformance tests for navigation_menu anatomy needed new assertions covering Sub* parts after they were added.

workflow: Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment via `gh pr comment`.
Review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation using the thread's PRRT_ ID.
Reply to individual review comments with fix references using `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr}/comments/{id}/replies`.
Polling CI with `gh pr checks` and PR state with GraphQL after each push is the standard loop for this project.
cargo insta test with INSTA_UPDATE=always generates new snapshots; fixtures should be inspected before committing.
Verifying spec compliance before changing public API (checking spec markdown directly) is the expected practice before acting on anatomy review findings.

friction: Multiple long sleep-based polling loops (120s, 240s, 300s) needed to wait for CI and Codex review to complete — no event-driven notification mechanism in use.
Codex review finding about nested-menu anatomy required manual cross-referencing against spec markdown before it could be acted on, adding lookup overhead.
Snapshot tests fail on first run for new parts (expected, but requires a separate INSTA_UPDATE=always pass before the normal test suite is clean).
open_items: Verify Codex processes the latest trigger (62ec346a) and produces no new findings — the session ended before the next Codex review cycle completed.
CI was restarted at the end of the session; final green status on all checks (clippy, mutual exclusion) was not confirmed.
